scope: Every active biblical or quasi-biblical book outside the Protestant canon, including deuterocanon, apocrypha, broader canon, additions, odes, and related active corpus files.
plainEnglishStandard: For every in-scope book and every translation lane, the app must either reproduce the correct source text at the correct displayed book/chapter/verse address, or omit that translation for that book/address because no legitimate source witness exists. The app must not synthesize missing translation text, silently shift source text to the wrong address, or treat governance markers as textual trust.
translationRule: Each translation lane present in the app must be independently trusted against its own source witness. A translation/book pair with no legitimate source witness is an allowed absence, not a blocker, provided the absence is explicit and non-synthetic.
userFacingTest: If a user opens a passage such as NRSV Tobit in the app and in the corresponding source Bible, the words must match at the displayed book/chapter/verse address. If the source Bible has no such book or verse for that translation, the app must not fake it.
variantRule: Variants, additions, alternate canonical forms, footnote-only readings, and source-form differences must be represented visibly through source-address mapping, variant apparatus, or explicit absence. They must not be hidden by forcing every source into one artificial row grid.
nextRequiredWork: Create a book-by-book and translation-by-translation collation matrix.
Classify every cell as exact-source-verified, source-absent-allowed, variant-handled, source-needed, or active-text-unverified.
Repair only cells that fail by source comparison, never by synthesis.
Promote trust only after the matrix has no failed or unverified active text cells.
